meaning: n.树林下的草丛,丛林,下层林丛
examples: A flashlight beam probed the underbrush only yards away from their hiding place.
Thick underbrush was growing there, sheltering thousands of insects and lizards.
The unintentional consequence has been to halt the natural eradication of underbrush, now the primary fuel for megafires.
Fire serves to clear out old trees and excess underbrush so younger trees can grow.
The rainforest made good on its name, pouring down until we hacked through the underbrush into a canopy-covered lagoon.
